Add full-journey.spec.ts E2E test that verifies both devices actively
edit game state and sync via SSE throughout a multi-night journey:

- Phase 1: Both devices load game, establish SSE connections
- Phase 2: Device B adds reminder token  Device A verifies
- Phase 3: Device A completes Night 1 with notes
- Phase 4: Device B kills player + adds drunk token  Device A verifies
- Phase 5: Device A adds poisoned token  Device B verifies
- Phase 6: Device B completes Night 2 with notes  Device A verifies
- Phase 7: Device B swaps player character  Device A verifies
- Phase 8: Device A kills another player  Device B verifies
- Phase 9: Device B completes Night 3 with notes  Device A verifies
- Phase 10: Final consistency check  both devices match API

commit.message:
fix: reset static downloadCounters between tests (#1009)

## Summary

- `PackageManagerService.downloadCounters` is a `private static`
property that persists across tests when running with `isolate: false`,
causing download count assertions to fail with inflated values
- Added `static resetDownloadCounters()` method to
`PackageManagerService` and call it in `afterEach` hook in
`test/.setup.ts`
- This ensures both database state (`truncateDatabase()`) and in-memory
state (`resetDownloadCounters()`) are cleaned up between tests

## Root Cause

1. Tests like `DownloadPackageVersionTarController.test.ts` download tgz
files, calling `plusPackageVersionCounter()` which accumulates counts in
the static `downloadCounters` object
2. These tests don't call
`runSchedule(SavePackageVersionDownloadCounter)`, so the counters remain
in memory
3. `afterEach → truncateDatabase()` clears the DB but not the static
variable
4. Later tests (e.g. `showPackageDownloads`) inherit stale counters →
assertions like `assert.equal(data.downloads[0].downloads, 6)` fail
because the actual value includes prior test accumulations

## Test plan

- [x] Verify the `showPackageDownloads` test passes in non-isolated mode
- [ ] Run full test suite to ensure no regressions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

feat: add segm support to synthetic dataset & benchmark coversion (#781)

- `draw_synthetic_shape` now returns `(img, polygon)`, ensuring the COCO polygon is generated from the same geometry used to render the shape (rendering and annotation remain synchronized). Documented as a breaking return-type change.

- `generate_synthetic_sample` stores polygons in `detections.data["polygons"]` without changing the public function signature. Fixed a critical bug by reshaping polygons with `.reshape(-1, 2)` after `np.asarray(polygon)` to prevent indexing errors when polygons are provided as flat lists.

- `generate_coco_dataset` adds `with_segmentation=False` to optionally produce segmentation annotations. Dataset splitting was corrected so the final split absorbs rounding remainders, preventing silent image drops.

- COCO export logic unified into `_write_coco_json(..., with_segmentation)` replacing two previous helpers. The function now computes annotation area from polygons when segmentation is enabled and raises `ValueError` if `file_paths` and `detections_list` lengths differ to avoid silent truncation.

- Added `_calculate_polygon_area` helper for computing COCO polygon areas.

- Added `synthetic_shape_segmentation_dataset_dir` pytest fixture for segmentation dataset generation.

- Reorganized `test_synthetic.py` into five focused test classes (39 tests) and expanded coverage for polygon area computation, shape edge cases, polygon–bbox consistency, dataset split correctness, and COCO writer validation (including mismatched inputs, missing polygons, and empty polygon fallback). Fixed invalid `>= pytest.approx(...)` comparisons.

- Updated documentation and comments: completed `generate_synthetic_sample` docstrings, added a Raises section to `_write_coco_json`, and clarified triangle geometry comments.

- Rewrote the segmentation convergence benchmark to use the current training stack (`RFDETRSegNanoConfig`, `SegmentationTrainConfig`, `RFDETRModule`, `build_trainer`), removing legacy engine references. Training now reads `num_classes` from dataset JSON, runs for 5 epochs, and validates convergence with bbox `mAP ≥ 20%` and segmentation `mAP ≥ 5%`.

- Standardized benchmark/tests: renamed `test_coco_inference.py` → `test_inference_coco.py` and `test_synthetic_convergence.py` → `test_training_synthetic.py`. COCO inference benchmarks now cap `num_workers` at 4, disable `compute_val_loss`, and reduce `det-nano` sample size to 1000.

commit.message:
Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern (#3561)

* Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern

The tvOS native build was combining device and simulator binaries into a
single fat framework at output/native/tvos/, while the NuGet packaging
only included runtimes/tvos/native/ — missing runtimes/tvossimulator/.
This caused linker errors when building for tvOS simulator.

Changes:
- native/tvos/build.cake: Split output into tvos/ and tvossimulator/
  directories, add ios_use_simulator flag, pass SKIA_PLATFORM property
- SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS.csproj: Add tvossimulator PackageFile
- HarfBuzzSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS.csproj: Add tvossimulator PackageFile

This mirrors the proven iOS build pattern that already correctly splits
device (ios/) and simulator (iossimulator/) native assets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix tvOS GnNinja to use min_tvos_version instead of min_ios_version

The tvOS build was incorrectly passing min_ios_version to GnNinja, which set
the iOS deployment target but left the tvOS deployment target at Skia's default
of 9.0. This changes it to min_tvos_version so the -mtvos-version-min flag is
set correctly to 11.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add missing fields to Go Game model for cross-device sync (#73)

The Go Game struct was missing 7 fields that exist in the TypeScript
Game type: activeFabled, activeLoric, inPlayCharacterIds, demonBluffs,
lunaticBluffs, playerBluffs, and customPlayerMessages.

When the UI pushed the full game object to the API, these fields were
silently dropped during JSON deserialization. This meant device B would
never see character selections, bluffs, or fabled/loric choices made
on device A.

commit.message:
fix: prevent SSE self-echo from closing bluff selection dialog (#71)

When a device pushes game state to the API, the server broadcasts a
version-changed SSE event to ALL connected clients  including the device
that triggered the save. Without a guard, the originating device would
fetch its own state back, dispatch SYNC_GAME, replace the game object,
and cause React to re-render, which resets UI state (e.g., closing the
DemonBluffSelection dialog ~3 seconds after it opens).

Add a 3-second cooldown after each local push. SSE version-changed events
arriving within this window are ignored, preventing the self-echo from
triggering unnecessary state replacement. Remote changes from other devices
arriving after the cooldown are still processed normally.

Co-authored-by: Dustin Schroeder <duschroe@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(supervisor): handle worker failure with lease reclaim and task recovery

Previously, the supervisor sent the terminal heartbeat (→ COMPLETING)
BEFORE checking the finalization result. COMPLETING is a terminal lease
state with no path to CRASHED or RECLAIMED, so failed workers left the
lease permanently stuck and the task stranded in ASSIGNED.

Now the supervisor checks the finalization outcome first:
- Success: sends terminal heartbeat → COMPLETING (unchanged)
- Failure: calls LeaseReclaimService to atomically transition the lease
  to CRASHED → RECLAIMED, evaluate retry/escalation policy, and recover
  the task (ASSIGNED → READY or FAILED based on retry budget)

The catch block (for workspace/runtime exceptions) also attempts lease
reclaim as best-effort recovery.

Adds LeaseReclaimerPort to WorkerSupervisorDependencies and
createReclaimUnitOfWork to the application adapters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.
